title: Employment Discrimination Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Employment Discrimination Assistance 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers No Filter Agricultural Laborers Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITY OF EUGENE CITY OF EUGENE OFFICE OF EQUITY & COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT HUMAN RIGHTS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fields discrimination complaints related to housing, employment, public access (including accessibility requests and grievances), and city contracts that occur within the City of Eugene. Also provides victim and community information and referrals in response to hate and bias activity. 500 East 4th Avenue Suite 101 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday by appointment only Email (541) 682-5177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY COMMISSION SEATTLE FIELD OFFICE EMPLOYMENT DISCRIMINATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces federal laws prohibiting discrimination in employment based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, age, disability, or genetic information. This includes discrimination involved in hiring, firing, promotions, harassment, training, wages, and benefits. Also works with Fair Employment Practice Agencies (FEPAs) and Tribal Employment Rights Offices (TEROs) to manage charges of discrimination and the protection of the employment rights of Native Americans. 909 First Avenue Suite 400 Seattle, WA 981041061 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-3pm Email (800) 669-4000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON FOREST GROVE FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 214-1384 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM FARMWORKER OFFICE FARMWORKER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to migrant and seasonal farmworkers with employment, housing, unemployment, and tax issues. Most employment and housing issues are related to payment of wages, unauthorized pay deductions, poor living conditions in labor camps, retaliation for asserting legal rights, poor health and safety conditions on the job, pesticide problems, and discrimination. Also gives referrals to other kinds of legal programs. Eligibility: Agricultural (including seasonal) workers who have experienced civil legal problems in the State of Oregon Hours: By appointment only (503) 981-5291 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces laws to ensure everyone has equal access at work, protects against discrimination, and shields workers who report safety problems or use leave. They also protect people who are looking for housing or using public places like stores and transportation. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 245-3844 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Enforces federal laws that protect people from discrimination based on race, color, national origin, disability status, sex, religion, familial status, or loss of other constitutional rights. Also enforces federal laws that protect individuals from discrimination by suing or prosecuting organizations or individuals who violate civil rights laws. These laws protect individuals from discrimination, harassment, and abuse in settings like housing, the workplace, school, voting, business, healthcare, and public spaces. 950 Pennsylvania Avenue NW Washington, DC 20530 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing discrimination on the basis of race, color, or ethnicity, disability, religion, sex, gender identity, and sexual orientation, immigration or citizenship status, language and/or national origin, family, marital or parental status, age, genetic identification, and service member status Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-2:30pm Email (855) 856-1247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF EUGENE HUMAN RIGHTS PROGRAM OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES CIVIL RIGHTS DIVISION
